concerns.text: Task T021 explicitly states 'Branching Factor is not calculated in this phase per Plan Phase 2 simplification.' However, Spec FR-003 mandates: 'The system MUST calculate the Average Branching Factor... and Global Connectivity'. This is a direct semantic weakening of a Functional Requirement. The task list fails to deliver a required metric, violating FR-003.

Task T038 requires calculating a 'linear_reasoning_index' to check for 'low branching' (avg out-degree < 1.5) to satisfy FR-008. However, T021 (the only metric calculation task) explicitly excludes Branching Factor. T038 cannot mathematically verify the 'low branching' condition without the metric defined in FR-003. This creates a logical impossibility where a required verification step (FR-008) is blocked by the omission of a required metric (FR-003).

Task T030 describes the search space as 'sweep all unique metric values in the success class subset'. Spec FR-004 mandates the threshold be 'the 20th percentile of the connectivity/branching distribution'. While T030 mentions '20th percentile' in its title, the description of 'sweeping all unique values' implies an optimization loop (finding the best F1) rather than the deterministic calculation of a specific percentile mandated by the spec. This risks semantic drift from the strict percentile rule to an optimization heuristic not authorized in FR-004.

Task T036 implements sensitivity analysis but does not explicitly list the required sweep values {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} and percentiles {10, 20, 30} mandated by SC-004. While it mentions 'sweep thresholds', the absence of the specific set in the task description risks implementation with arbitrary defaults, failing to satisfy the specific acceptance criteria of SC-004.
